summary: Normalizes an optional parsing configuration object into a complete options object using defaults and validating supported decoder and charset settings.
signature_intent: Accepts an optional options object and returns either the default options object or a normalized options object for parsing behavior.
behavior: Returns the shared default options when no options object is provided.
Rejects a non-null, non-undefined decoder value unless it is a function.
Rejects a charset value unless it is undefined, utf-8, or iso-8859-1.
Uses the default charset when charset is omitted.
Normalizes boolean-like dot support with truthiness when provided, otherwise uses the default.
Uses provided boolean values for prototype, sentinel, comma, numeric entity, plain object, and strict null handling flags; otherwise uses defaults.
Uses provided numeric limits for arrays and parameters; otherwise uses defaults.
Uses a provided decoder function; otherwise uses the default decoder.
Uses a provided string or regular-expression delimiter; otherwise uses the default delimiter.
Accepts numeric depth or false and coerces it to a number; otherwise uses the default depth.
Enables query-prefix ignoring only when explicitly true.
Enables array parsing unless explicitly false.
edge_cases: Null or undefined options return defaults without validation.
Null or undefined decoder values are allowed and fall back to the default decoder.
Unsupported charset values throw a TypeError.
A false depth value is accepted and becomes numeric zero.
Non-boolean values for most boolean flags fall back to defaults, except dot support is coerced by truthiness and query-prefix ignoring requires true.
Array parsing remains enabled for any value other than false.
forbidden_details: No variable names, repository names, source paths, or incidental implementation quirks are included.
